comments.body:
> Really nice improvements, LGTM
> 
> > [FAILED] DeferCleanup callback returned error: unlinkat /tmp/ginkgo349969710/root/vfs/dir/b993cfcfd8fdc2731b308222fce8b5ce7a681780ab276b6a23e1b0fe3ce06bbb/bin: permission denied
> 
> Not yet sure where that's coming from.

That is the classic problem that as rootless you cannot delete files owned by subuid/gid without joining the userns. We have to use `podman unshare rm -rf` in that case and I thought I covered that, running locally passed because it used the images from my local cache so I missed that one

> That is the classic problem that as rootless you cannot delete files owned by subuid/gid without joining the userns.

That I see but I fail to see where this was removed in the PR.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/hold

I need time to understand the `ImageCache` changes. My home network is ultra-slow, and if I have to re-pull images each time, I will never be able to run ginkgo again

> I need time to understand the ImageCache changes. My home network is ultra-slow, and if I have to re-pull images each time, I will never be able to run ginkgo again

You don't have to repull, ImageCache is the path were the extracted images are stored not the cached tar files. That location is still the same.

>You don't have to repull, [...] cached tar files. That location is still the same.

Confirmed. Thank you. Thank you for doing this, and extra thanks for fixing the `TMPDIR/root` and `TMPDIR/runroot` bug, that one always annoyed me!

All other commits LGTM, and I've verified that timings in run logs look reasonable (at a glance).

/hold cancel

Uh-oh. @Luap99 I didn't notice this when I reviewed logs, but my flake logger did: [this PR flaked](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5642322990333952/html/int-podman-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html), in fetching cache images, NBD that happens all the time because of flaky quay. However:
```
[+0203s] [SynchronizedAfterSuite] [FAILED] [0.052 seconds]
...
           [FAILED] Unexpected error:
               <*fs.PathError | 0xc0004c5e90>: 
               open /timings-1: no such file or directory
               {
                   Op: "open",
                   Path: "/timings-1",
                   Err: <syscall.Errno>0x2,
```
The `/timings-1` is once again missing `LockTmpDir`.

> Uh-oh. @Luap99 I didn't notice this when I reviewed logs, but my flake logger did: [this PR flaked](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5642322990333952/html/int-podman-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html), in fetching cache images, NBD that happens all the time because of flaky quay. However:
> 
> ```
> [+0203s] [SynchronizedAfterSuite] [FAILED] [0.052 seconds]
> ...
>            [FAILED] Unexpected error:
>                <*fs.PathError | 0xc0004c5e90>: 
>                open /timings-1: no such file or directory
>                {
>                    Op: "open",
>                    Path: "/timings-1",
>                    Err: <syscall.Errno>0x2,
> ```
> 
> The `/timings-1` is once again missing `LockTmpDir`.

Yes that is fine and the failure mode we had locally before. SynchronizedAfterSuite will run even when SynchronizedBeforeSuite fails. Which means we can end up with an unset LockTmpDir. But in this case we only open for reading so it should no longer create the files.
